feedback.text: I and many others would really like for videogames to be included in the 'digital fairness act' to hinder the destruction of videogames and even the insane number of "gambling a-like" microtransactions. One of my all time favorite experiences in my young life is the completion of the videogame "The Crew" which acted as catalyst for the 'Stop destroying videogames' movement. The videogame "The Crew" is now fully unable to be played at all, and if you owned a digital copy, it has been taken from you by the developers, physical copies still exist (of course) but they are unable to be played as servers are needed but have been taken down. The game in it self really doesn't need any online at all to be played with the exception that the developers just decided that it would be needed when you can play it with many people in a big world but you can fully play it by yourself. There was really no reason except for that they themselves were so 'not forward thinking' to make the game always need an online server. And who suffered ? The people that gave their money to that company. That's all i have to say.
